title: Sliding Window — ROI & Pattern
oneSentence: Avoid recalculating overlapping subarray values by sliding a window.
description: Trace the sliding window templates (Fixed & Variable length) and learn the core steps of sliding windows.

roi.complexity: Time: O(N) linear time | Space: O(1) or O(K) for state

codeLines.text: public static int solveFixed(int[] nums, int k) {
for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) windowSum += nums[i];
for (int i = k; i < nums.length; i++) {
windowSum -= nums[i - k]; // Left exits -> subtract
public static int solveVariable(int[] nums, int target) {
// Shrink window left while condition is violated

traceSteps.title: 1. Build Initial Window
traceSteps.text: Process elements from 0 to K-1 to establish our first window state.

traceSteps.title: 2. Slide Loop
traceSteps.text: Loop from index K to the end of the array, introducing one element at a time.

traceSteps.title: 3. Expand Window Right
traceSteps.text: Add the new incoming element `nums[i]` to our active window tracking.

traceSteps.title: 4. Shrink Window Left
traceSteps.text: Deduct the outgoing element `nums[i - k]` that is no longer inside the glass.

traceSteps.title: 5. Track Maximum State
traceSteps.text: Update the best state variable (e.g. maxSum) with the current window value.

traceSteps.title: Variable Window Concept
traceSteps.text: In a variable window, we dynamically expand the right boundary and contract the left boundary depending on condition validity.

traceSteps.title: Expand Boundary Loop
traceSteps.text: Iterate the right pointer from 0 to the end of the array to expand the window's right edge.

traceSteps.title: Expand State Update
traceSteps.text: Add the element at the right pointer to currentSum (or update window state frequency maps).

traceSteps.title: Shrink Violation Loop
traceSteps.text: While the window's state violates constraints (e.g., currentSum exceeds target), shrink the window from the left.

traceSteps.title: Shrink State Update
traceSteps.text: Subtract the element at the left pointer from currentSum and advance the left pointer.

traceSteps.title: Valid Window Tracking
traceSteps.text: Once the constraints are satisfied, track the optimal window length (right - left + 1).

sandbox.question: For a fixed window of size K, how do we initialize our window state?
options: Sum elements from index 0 to K-1
Loop through the entire array first

sandbox.question: When sliding a fixed window of size K at index `i`, which element is removed?
Where do you update the tracker for a SHORTEST variable window?
options: Inside the shrink loop (while window is valid)
At the very end of the main function

followUps.q: If the window size isn't fixed, how do you decide when to shrink the left side?
followUps.a: You use a `while` loop to shrink `left` as long as the current window violates the problem constraint (e.g., duplicate characters found, sum exceeded).

followUps.q: What if you need to track frequency of elements within the window?
followUps.a: Use a HashMap (or an integer array of size 26 for letters) to maintain counts of elements currently in the window.

followUps.q: How would you handle 'at most K unique elements' or 'at most K replacements'?
followUps.a: Keep a frequency map. When `map.size() > K`, shrink the window from the left until `map.size() <= K`. For replacements, track `max_freq` and ensure `window_size - max_freq <= K`.

followUps.q: Can you optimize space if input is limited to lowercase letters?
followUps.a: Yes, use a fixed `int[26]` array instead of a generic HashMap to achieve true O(1) auxiliary space and faster memory access.

followUps.q: Can you do this in a single pass?
followUps.a: Yes, standard Sliding Window guarantees O(N) time because both `right` and `left` pointers only move forward, visiting each element at most twice.
